gold_value: 12.1.5.3 Should there be savings from any particular line item once that line item buyout is complete, the Contractor shall reallocate the savings into a  separate line item designated as “Buyout Savings”. This “Buyout Savings” shall function as the aggregate repository for use in balancing line items until buyout is complete. The Buyout Savings is to be exhausted prior to the Contractor requesting the Agent’s approval for any reallocation of the construction contingency.

question: What is the Contingency Usage Article #?
What are the Contingency Usage Terms and Conditions?
gold_value:
The Contingency, when agreed upon by the Owner and the Contractor as set forth below and subject to the conditions in this Section 5.2.5, shall be an amount available to reimburse the Contractor for work not reasonably identified or reasonably quantified by the Contractor in its cost estimate, such as: (a) estimating and purchasing errors; (b) premium time to maintain the progress 
of the Work in accordance with the Project schedule due to an action or inaction by the Contractor or any Subcontractor (provided that the Contractor shall require Subcontractors to absorb in their base Subcontracts all premium time due to failure of the Subcontractor to properly man the Project or organize the Work); (c) unforeseen construction problems (except to the extent that, as a contractor experienced in the construction of projects similar to the Project, the Contractor should have foreseen such problems); (d) extrordinary or unusual seasonal weather conditions; and (e) Work not identified or reasonably inferable from the Contract Documents, but not completely detailed in such documents.

What is the Adverse Weather Days Article #?
What are the Adverse Weather Days Terms & Conditions?
gold_value: 8.3.5 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in any of the Contract Documents, the Contractor acknowledges and agrees that no extension of time shall be granted on account of adverse weather conditions except as provided for in this paragraph. For purposes of this Agreement, "adverse weather" is defined as when because of weather conditions: (1) a state emergency or other declaration is made by the Governor of Pennsylvania directing non-essential workers to not report to work; or (2) Work cannot safely progress including, without limitation, winds that exceed the applicable manufacturer’s recommendation or local regulation for the safe use of a hoist, crane or other equipment (3) such weather conditons exist that are are unordinary and that significantly hinder the performance of the Work.  Written notice of adverse weather must be delivered by the Contractor to the Owner within five (5) days following such adverse weather and if the Contractor fails to so deliver written notice of adverse weather to the Owner by such time, the Contractor shall have no right to seek an extension of the Contract Time on account of such adverse weather.

What is the Substantial Completion Article #?
What are the Substantial Completion Terms & Conditions?
gold_value:
9.8.1 Substantial Completion of the entire Work shall be achieved when (a) the Work is sufficiently complete in accordance with the Contract Documents so the Owner can commence beneficial occupancy of the Project, (b) the Architect has issued a Certificate of Substantial Completion as provided in Section 9.8.4 below, (c) the Contractor has obtained all government approvals required or necessary to use or occupy the space where all or any of the Work was performed including, but not limited to, either a final, unconditional certificate of occupancy or a temporary certificate of occupancy that permits occupancy of the entire Project and is subject to conditions acceptable to the Owner, in the Owner’s reasonable judgment, and (d) the Owner, Contractor and Architect have 
agreed upon the punch-list.

However, the completion of the Elevator is excluded.  The County and Fire Marshall will NOT issue a TCO or CO.  In addition, our qualifications states "Proposal assumes completion without Certificate of Occupancy" and "The current project design is not adequate to achieve Beneficial Occupancy and Certificate of Occupancy" and "TI contractor is responsible to provide Beneficial Occupancy and Certificate of Occupancy."

gold_value: 4.3.4 Contractor agrees to and shall achieve full and final completion of the entire Work, including, but not limited to, punch list, clean up and demobilization (“Final Completion”), within a reasonable time after Substantial Completion, but in all events within Thirty (30) days thereafter (the “Final Completion Date”).

question: What is the Article # for IMC's Stop Work Ability for late Owner payment?
What are the Terms & Conditions for IMC's Stop Work Ability for late Owner payment?
gold_value:
9.7 Failure of Payment 
If the Owner does not pay the Contractor within seven days after the date established in the Contract Documents the amount approved by the Owner pursuant to Section 9.5.1, then the Contractor may, upon seven additional days’ notice to the Owner and Architect, stop the Work until payment of the amount owing has been received. The Contract Time shall be extended appropriately and the Contract Sum shall be increased by the amount of the Contractor’s reasonable costs of shutdown, delay and start-up, plus interest as provided for in the Contract 
Documents.
